data.title: SEPC Bags New Contract Worth INR6.50 Billion; Shares Down 6%
data.description:
Engineering, procurement, and construction company SEPC , has been awarded a contract worth 6.50 billion Indian rupees from Parmeshi Urja, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The contract involved design, engineering, supply, installation, and commissioning of a 133 MW solar power project, including all associated civil and structural works, across four districts of Maharashtra, India.

The project is expected to be executed in 12-18 months from the start of the project.

The company's shares were down nearly 6% in recent trade.

data.title: Spanish PM's far-left ally wants 'reset' after corruption case
data.description:
Spain's Labour Minister Yolanda Diaz, who leads the ruling coalition's far-left junior party Sumar, called for a "reset" of government on Friday after a corruption investigation forced out a close aide of the prime minister.

Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez has apologised to Spaniards and promised an investigation of his Socialist Party but also resisted critics' calls for a snap election after Thursday's resignation of the party's number three Santos Cerdan.

A Supreme Court judge has asked Cerdan to testify over accusations, which he denies, of being involved in the awarding of public works contracts for kickbacks.

The fragile government has faced a series of scandals including a high-profile probe into whether Sanchez's wife Begona Gomez used her status to sway business dealings.

"This legislature needs an authentic reset," Diaz told reporters. "We will demand right now a meeting of the government coalition ... the only possible way out is a U-turn to take the legislature towards social policies."

Sumar pulls the coalition policies leftwards, pressing for less spending on defence and stronger worker protection, for instance.

"Forgiveness is not enough," Diaz added, without specifying further what she wanted the government to do.

Sanchez's coalition struggles to get any initiatives, such as budget bills, approved by lawmakers.

A sombre-looking prime minister said in his address late on Thursday that he regretted trusting Cerdan.

He took over as prime minister in 2018 after the biggest corruption investigation in Spain's democratic history netted scores of people linked to the then ruling People's Party, leading to the ejection of Prime Minister Mariano Rajoy.

Since then and after two elections in 2019 and 2023, Sanchez has ruled in minority coalitions.

data.title: Shein's planned Hong Kong listing to benefit from wider capital pool, analysts say
data.description:
Key points:
  • Shein faces scrutiny over supply chains and cotton sourcing
  • Hong Kong listing could avoid UK protests and political pushback
  • Hong Kong IPO offers Stock Connect access for mainland investors

Shein's planned listing in Hong Kong will help the online fast-fashion retailer avoid sharp investor scrutiny of its supply chains while tapping into capital from the mainland and emerging market investors, analysts said.

The Singapore-headquartered company has turned its public market debut ambitions to Hong Kong after failing to win Chinese securities regulatory approval to proceed with a London initial public offering, Reuters reported last month, citing sources.

While a listing, if successful, would be a big boost for Hong Kong, the move would cast a cloud over the company's efforts in recent years to gain legitimacy as a global, rather than a Chinese company.

Shein, which sells products including $5 bike shorts and $18 sundresses, has faced political and environmental group pressure in the UK over its cotton sourcing and supply chain practices.

It has also faced allegations that its clothes contain cotton from China's Xinjiang region, where the U.S. and NGOs have accused the Chinese government of human rights abuses and forced labour. Beijing denies any abuses.

The company, which moved its headquarters from China to Singapore in 2022, has previously said it has a zero-tolerance policy for forced labour and requires its contract manufacturers to only source cotton from approved regions.

"If it is the only option now open to them, the Hong Kong market does make sense as a place where you could list a global business with a mainland supply chain," said Eliot Fisk, a Hong Kong capital markets consultant and former JPMorgan banker.

Shein did not respond to a Reuters request for comment.

Before its attempt to list in London, Shein had pursued a listing in New York. The China-founded company had also faced regulatory hurdles and pushback from U.S. lawmakers in its attempt to list in the United States.

"Listing in Hong Kong would also likely dodge the protests and political pushback it might face in the UK," said Craig Coben, former Bank of America co-head of capital markets in Hong Kong.

While it is not known whether Shein plans to seek any waivers for a potential Hong Kong listing, several waivers, including disclosure-related waivers, can be sought by large IPO hopefuls in the Asian financial hub, according to capital market lawyers.

A Hong Kong listing would also allow Shein to eventually be added to the city's Stock Connect scheme which gives easier access for mainland and Hong Kong-based investors to buy shares on each country's respective markets more easily.

Shein would easily meet the market capitalisation and other criteria for inclusion in the connect scheme and for attracting mainland investment, said Hong Kong-based advisory firm Emmer Capital Partners CEO Manishi Raychaudhuri.

There was a 255% year-on-year increase in average daily turnover in the first three months of the year in Southbound trading, mainland investors buying and selling Hong Kong stocks, the Hong Kong Exchange said in its first quarter results.

"Hong Kong would have a dominant presence of Asia and emerging market-focused investors. London on the other hand, would have a significant presence of global and developed market investors," Raychaudhuri said.

"The supply chain issues would have been a more important consideration for the latter set of investors."

($1 = 7.8488 Hong Kong dollars)

data.title: Stronger Demand for Quality Homes Likely to Boost Price Growth in China — Market Talk
data.description: Stronger demand from upgraders for quality new homes will likely boost price growth in China, HSBC analysts say in a research note. HSBC says that the housing market is finally shifting back to a market-oriented model that allows home prices to appreciate based on genuine supply-demand dynamics. Upgrader demand accounts for about half of new home sales, HSBC says. "This market is being driven by upgrades--modern design, higher efficiency ratios, higher ceilings--along with sophisticated amenities," the analysts say. These buyers are looking for prices that are fair, with room for appreciation, they add. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: JcbNext Cuts Stake in Associate
data.description:
JcbNext disposed of 647,846 shares in its associate 104 Corp for NT$146 million by selling them in the open market.

The shares were sold between Dec. 28, 2024, to June 12, 2025, and represent 1.95% of 104's issued shares.

The disposal decreased JcbNext's shareholdings in 104 Corp from 4.5 million shares to 3.8 million shares, representing 11.5% of the latter's issued share capital.

data.title: Fitch Keeps Ferrovial's Rating, Outlook on Robust Financial Structure
data.description:
Fitch on Thursday affirmed the BBB long-term issuer default rating of Ferrovial , with a stable outlook.

The rating agency anticipates that the Spanish transport infrastructure company will maintain "prudent" balance sheet management and steady earnings at 2024 levels, backed by an expected boost in net dividends from concessions and positive cash flow generation from construction projects.

Fitch also noted Ferrovial's strong market position and pipeline of order backlog and orders, which ensure a solid revenue outlook over the next two to three years.

data.title: Xinxin Mining Completes Acquisition of 51% Stake in Huaou Mining
data.description:
Xinjiang Xinxin Mining said it completed the acquisition of a 51% stake in Xinjiang Huaou Mining on June 12, according to a same-day Hong Kong bourse filing.

Following the transaction, Huaou Mining has become a non-wholly owned subsidiary, and its financial results will be consolidated into the group's statements, the company said.

data.title: Spring Airlines's May Passenger Trips Carried Up 14.6% Y/Y
data.description:
Spring Airlines Co Ltd :

  • SAYS MAY PASSENGER TRIPS CARRIED UP 14.6% Y/Y, MAIL AND CARGO CARRIED UP 22.5% Y/Y

data.title: Archer Aviation Raises $850 Million in Direct Offering
data.description:
Archer Aviation said late Thursday it raised $850 million in a registered direct offering of 85 million shares at $10 per share.

The company said the financing came after the executive order by US President Donald Trump last week to launch a pilot program to accelerate the use of electric vertical take-off and landing aircraft in the country.

The company said it plans to use net proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes, "with a focus on the build out of Archer's commercial capabilities."

Archer said it also plans to coordinate with the White House, Department of Transportation and the Federal Aviation Administration on Archer's plans to ramp up its operations in the US.

data.title: Chinney Investments to Widen Full-Year Loss
data.description:
Chinney Investments expects an attributable loss of between HK$560 million and HK$580 million for the year ended March 31, up from HK$66.7 million a year prior, a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The property company attributed the forecast to a deferral in the recognition of contract sales from a residential project in Guangzhou, net fair value losses from revaluations of its investment properties, and amortization of goodwill.

data.title: Italian Stocks Fall to 3-Week Low
data.description:
The FTSE MIB fell to around 39,200 on Friday, hitting its lowest level in over three weeks, tracking a global sell-off amid rising Middle East tensions.

Israeli airstrikes on Iran marked a significant escalation in regional conflict, with Israel declaring a state of emergency, raising fears of imminent Iranian retaliation.

Meanwhile, investors are also monitoring trade developments, with details on the US-China framework still pending, as President Trump plans to set tariff rates for trading partners in the coming weeks.

Across the bourse, major stocks posted sharp losses, including UniCredit (-2.6%), Intesa Sanpaolo (-1.6%), Enel (-1%), Ferrari (-2.1%), and Generali (-1.9%).

On the upside, energy company Eni rose 1.6%, supported by rising oil prices amid supply disruption fears, while defense group Leonardo gained 1.5%.

For the week, the index is poised for its worst performance since late March.

data.title: Israel's Attacks Lift South Korean Defense Stocks
data.description:
By Kwanwoo Jun

Shares of guided-missile maker LIG Nex1 and most South Korean defense stocks rallied amid growing Middle East tension after Israel's attacks on Iran.

Israel launched a series of early Friday strikes on Iran's nuclear and military targets, killing the head of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard. That prompted Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei to say Israel "should expect severe punishment" for the attacks.

The escalating Israel-Iran tension and risk-off sentiment sent the benchmark Kospi 0.9% lower at 2894.62 on Friday, snapping a seven-session winning streak.

However, investors piled into local defense stocks, as the flare-up in the Middle East tensions raised expectations of strong demand for security and military spending there.

LIG Nex1 surged 14% to close at 526,000 won, equivalent to $388.09 on Friday, standing out among local defense shares which were mostly higher.

The South Korean maker of precision-guided munitions has supplied surface-to-air missiles to the United Arab Emirates, Saudi Arabia and Iraq for their growing air defense needs in recent years.

Daiwa Capital analysts Mike Oh and Daeho Son said in a recent note that they expect LIG Nex1's annual backlog-order growth be 15% this year, accelerating from 2% in 2024. They raised their 2025 revenue forecast the company by 3% to 4.0 trillion won, citing the increased revenues from precision-guided munitions projects in the Middle East.

Meanwhile, shares of armored-vehicle supplier Hyundai Rotem ended 4.0% higher at 186,800 won and fighter-jet manufacturer Korea Aerospace Industries finished 8.0% higher at 97,700 won. Self-propelled howitzer maker Hanwha Aerospace, however, slipped 0.8% to close at 945,000 won.

Write to Kwanwoo Jun at kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com

data.title: Market Chatter: Wheelock Project Rakes in HK$7.5 Billion from Sale of 1,207 Units
data.description:
Wheelock Properties raked in HK$7.5 billion from the sale of 1,207 units at phase 12 of Lohas Park in Tseung Kwan O, Hong Kong, The Standard reported Thursday.

The units comprise more than 60% of the total 1,985 units, the report said.

Lohas Park is jointly developed by Wheelock and MTR , the report said.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: SyntheticMR Names Permanent CEO
data.description:
SyntheticMR (SYNT.ST) named Lena Åredal as chief executive officer to succeed acting CEO Johanna Norén.

Åredal, who currently serves as CEO of Swedish medtech company Perimed, will take on her new role by Dec. 15, the quantitative imaging software company said Friday.

Norén will resume her chief financial role following the CEO appointment.

data.title: Slovakia Inflation Rate Picks Up in May
data.description:
Slovakia’s annual inflation rate increased to 4.1% in May 2025 from a four-month low of 3.7% in the previous month, in line with market expectations.

The upturn was driven by higher prices across several key categories, including food and non-alcoholic beverages (3.9% vs 3.6% in April), housing and utilities (2.8% vs 2.5%), furnishings, household equipment and routine household maintenance (3.5% vs 2.8%), alcoholic beverages and tobacco (4.9% vs 3.2%), health (2.0% vs 1.9%), recreation and culture (5.0% vs 4.7%), and miscellaneous goods and services (6.5% vs 5.9%).

At the same time, prices rebounded for transport (0.1% vs -1.6%).

On the other hand, inflation eased slightly for clothing and footwear (3.4% vs 3.6%), while prices remained unchanged for communication (6.1%) and education (10.2%).

On a monthly basis, consumer prices increased by 0.5% in May, from 0.1% in the prior month.

data.title: Alibaba Adjusts Conversion Rate for 2031 Bonds; Shares Down 3%
data.description:
Alibaba Group adjusted the conversion rate of its $4.5 billion convertible bonds due 2031 following the declaration of dividends, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The rate was revised from 9.7271 to 9.8915 ADSs per $1,000 principal amount. The maximum conversion rate, reflecting make-whole provisions, was also raised from 12.6452 to 12.8589 ADSs, effective June 12.

Following the adjustment, the maximum number of ordinary shares issuable upon full conversion increased to 514.36 million, from 505.81 million, the filing said.

Shares of the tech giant were down about 3% in afternoon trade on Friday.

data.title: UK Stocks Pull Back Off Record Highs
data.description:
The FTSE 100 fell by 0.6% to around 8,830 in early trading on Friday, retreating from yesterday's record closing high, as rising tensions in the Middle East triggered a broad-based sell-off across global equity markets.

Israel launched widescale strikes against Iran, marking a major escalation in tensions between the two countries and fueled fears of a broader conflict in the region.

Meanwhile, investors are closely watching trade developments, with details yet to be determined on US-China framework agreement, while US President Donald Trump said he would be setting tariff rates for trading partners over the next two to three weeks.

Losses were widespread, with Barclays (-2.3%), Natwest (-2.2%), and Antofagasta (-2%) among the biggest decliners.

data.title: Q & M Dental Group (Singapore) Buys Back Shares; Shares Slip 3%
data.description:
Q & M Dental Group (Singapore) bought back 707,800 shares in the open market on Thursday for SG$284,246, according to a same-day filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The company has been authorized to buy back around 94.9 million shares under its current mandate. To date, it has bought back 1.7 million shares.

data.title: Lifecare ASA: Notification of trade - exercise of warrants
data.description:
Reference is made to the stock exchange announcement by Lifecare ASA (the "Company") dated 2 June 2025 regarding the commencement of the exercise period for 4,193,806 warrants (the "Warrants") issued in connection with the partially underwritten rights issue of new shares in the Company completed in June 2024 (the "Rights Issue").

Each Warrant gives the holder a right to subscribe for one new share in the Company at an exercise price per share of NOK 5.31681. Warrants that are not exercised to subscribe for new shares before the end of the Exercise Period today 13 June 2025 at 16:30 hours (CEST) will have no value and will lapse without compensation to the holder. Holders of Warrants who do not exercise their Warrants within the Exercise Period may experience a dilution of their shareholding in the Company.

The Company has been informed that a major shareholder of Lifecare ASA, Tjelta AS, has exercised 129,139 Warrants, for the allocation of 129,139 New Shares in the Company at a price per New Share of NOK 5.31681.

Subject to timely payment of the aggregate exercise amount for the new shares on 19 June 2025, the Company expects that the share capital increase pertaining to the issuance of the new shares will be registered with the Norwegian Register of Business Enterprises on or about 24 June 2025 and that shares will be delivered to the VPS accounts of the subscribers to whom they are allocated on or about 25 June 2025.

The shares received upon exercise of Warrants will be listed on Euronext Oslo Børs under ISIN NO0013355859 and ticker code "LIFE". The shares will be listed as soon as the new shares are fully paid, the share capital increase pertaining to the issuance of the new shares has been registered in the Norwegian Register of Business Enterprises, and the new shares have been issued in the VPS.

The shares received upon exercise of Warrants may not be transferred or traded before (i) the new shares have been fully paid, (ii) the share capital increase pertaining to the issuance of the new shares has been registered with the Norwegian Register of Business Enterprises, and (iii) the new shares have been issued in the VPS.

data.title: India stocks, rupee, bonds swaps, call at 1:00 P.M. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was down 0.8% at 81,033, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 fell 0.8% to 24680, dragged by oil and gas stocks, as Israel's military strikes on Iran deepened tensions in the oil-rich Middle East and triggered a broad-based selloff across markets.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee fell 0.6% versus the U.S. dollar at 86.1100, as a surge in oil prices after Israel's strikes on Iran sparked a wave of risk aversion across markets, prompting the central bank to step in and limit further losses, traders said.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN063335G=CC) was quoted at 100.10 rupees, with the yield over 3 basis points higher at 6.3151%, as higher oil prices and caution before the weekly debt auction dampened sentiment.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was down 1 basis point at 5.53%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) was up 2 basis points at 5.75%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5.34%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.12%.

data.title: GDB Chairman Resigns; Successor Named
data.description:
GDB Holdings redesignated its independent director, Chia Lui Meng, as nonexecutive chairman on Thursday, according to a same-day Malaysian bourse filing.

He will succeed Zaini Bin Omar in the role after the latter resigned because of health issues, according to a separate filing.

data.title: DBS Buys Back, Cancels Shares Worth Over SG$13 Million
data.description:
DBS Group bought back and canceled 300,000 shares worth SG$13.5 million in the open market on Thursday, according to a same-day filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The bank has been authorized to buy back around 85.3 million shares under its current mandate. To date, it has bought back 5.8 million shares.

data.title: Oil Soars as Israeli Strike on Iran Sparks Fears of Supply Disruptions
data.description:
By Giulia Petroni

Oil futures gained more than $4 a barrel on Friday after Israel launched a wide-ranging attack against Iran's nuclear facilities and military leadership, stoking fears of regional conflict that could disrupt global energy flows.

In early European trade, Brent crude rose 5.5% to $73.18 a barrel, while the U.S. oil gauge West Texas Intermediate was up 5.5% to $71.76 a barrel. The benchmarks surged as much as 13% before pairing gains.

"The market's response to last night's attack has been very strong," said Ipek Ozkardeskaya, senior analyst at Swissquote Bank. "Prices have since pulled back slightly, but tensions are far from over."

Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said the operation would continue for "as many days as it takes," and Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei warned Israel "should expect severe punishment" for the strikes.

The attack marks a significant escalation in regional tensions, raising the specter of a broader Middle East conflict that could severely disrupt both regional and global oil and gas supplies.

"This has elevated geopolitical uncertainty significantly and requires the oil market to price in a larger risk premium for any potential supply disruptions," said Warren Patterson, ING's head of commodity strategy.

Iran is a major oil producer and holds a strategic position on the northern side of the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial route through which about a third of global seaborne oil flows.

"What impact this would have on the oil market depends on whether downstream assets are affected or if midstream and upstream assets are targeted," Patterson said. "The latter would have more of an impact on the global market, putting as much as 1.7 million barrels a day of export supply at risk."

The strikes come just days before U.S. special envoy Steve Witkoff was due to meet his Iranian counterparts in Oman for a sixth round of nuclear talks. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the U.S. wasn't involved in the strikes.

Write to Giulia Petroni at giulia.petroni@wsj.com

data.title: Gold Futures Rally as Israel Launches Strikes on Iran — Market Talk
data.description: Gold futures rise as Middle East tensions boil over. Futures are up 1% at $3,437.80 a troy ounce. Markets have shifted into a firm risk-off sentiment after Israel launched strikes on Iran's nuclear facility overnight, RBC Capital Markets analysts say in a note. Israel's strikes also killed the head of Iran's Revolutionary Guard and leading nuclear scientists, with Iran launching a drone counter strike this morning. Demand for safe-haven assets like gold have surged in response, RBC says. The precious metal is nearing its all-time high of $3,509.90/oz, set in mid-April. U.S. and European equity futures are losing ground alongside cryptocurrencies as investors shift funds into lower risk assets, RBC says. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

data.title: Lectra Appoints Maximilien Abadie As Deputy CEO
data.description:
Lectra SA :

Source text:

Further company coverage:

data.title: Towngas to Launch Home Safety Alert System for Elderly Residents
data.description:
Hong Kong and China Gas and the Hong Kong Federation of Trade Unions will pilot its home safety improvement program for elderly residents, the gas company said Thursday.

The Gas Guardian Care Network will monitor gas usage records and send alerts to caregivers if no gas usage is recorded from elderly residents' homes for two straight days, the report said.

data.title: B&S International Warns of Up to 50% Slump in Full-Year Profit
data.description:
B&S International expects a 40% to 50% year-on-year decline in net profit for the year ended March 31, a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The food and beverage distributor attributed the forecast to a drop in revenue and gross profit in the retail and distribution business wings amid a weakened consumer market in Hong Kong.

data.title: Escalating Middle East Tensions Weigh Down Chinese Stocks; Western Region Gold Jumps 10%
data.description:
Chinese shares tracked the losses of their global peers on Friday, as market appetite for risky assets diminished amid the escalation of conflict in the Middle East.

The Shanghai Composite Index, the main gauge of Chinese stocks, fell 0.8% to 3,377.00. The Shenzhen Component Index dropped 1.1% to 10,122.11.

Israeli airstrikes in Iran earlier on Friday resulted in the death of the chief of the Iranian Armed Forces and the commander-in-chief of Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps as well as two of the country's top nuclear scientists.

On the domestic front, the Chinese government is looking to boost its cooperation with the European Central Bank, including the reform of the international monetary system.

China will also step up reviews of export license applications for rare-earth-related items, according to the Ministry of Commerce. Spokesperson He Yadong said that while some licenses have already been approved, controls remain vital due to the dual-use nature of rare earths in both military and civilian sectors.

In company news, Western Region Gold plans to acquire Xinjiang Meisheng Mining for 1.66 billion yuan. Shares of the gold smelter jumped 10% Friday.

data.title: Swiss Franc Rises as Geopolitical Tensions Boost Safe Havens — Market Talk
data.description: The Swiss franc rises after Israel launched an attack on Iran's nuclear facilities. The rising geopolitical tensions fuel demand for safe-haven assets including the franc. "The situation between Iran and Israel is likely to dominate price action on Friday," XTB's Kathleen Brooks says in a note. Any escalation could be met by a surge in safe havens and commodities, she says. The euro falls 0.2% to 0.9368 Swiss francs, having reached a three-week low of 0.9305 francs overnight, according to LSEG. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)
